inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** тАФ Guard removal creates behavior regression when outDir is nested inside publicDir тАФ The deleted `copyDir` in `utils.ts` contained a guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) that skipped entries inside `publicDir` whose resolved path equals `outDir` тАФ the case where `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir` (e.g. `publicDir: 'public'`, `outDir: 'public/build'`). The old behavior silently skipped the nested outDir entry. `fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true })` does not reproduce this: when the destination is inside the source, `cpSync` throws `ERR_FS_CP_DIR_TO_SUBDIRECTORY`. This converts a previously-successful config into a hard error during `closeBundle`. Either confirm an upstream guard exists in `prepareOutDirPlugin`, or replicate the check before calling `cpSync`.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** тАФ fs.cpSync is experimental on supported Node versions (< 22.3.0) тАФ eslint-disable silences the real concern тАФ `fs.cpSync` carried Stability:1 (Experimental) status and emitted a runtime `ExperimentalWarning` until Node 22.3.0. Vite declares support for Node 18/20/22, and create-vite runs on whatever Node the user has. Users on Node 18.xтАУ22.2 will see `ExperimentalWarning: fs.cpSync is an experimental feature` on every build's public-dir copy and every scaffolding run. The eslint-disable comment ('it is not experimental in Node 22.3+') is true but doesn't address the supported-versions floor. Either bump the engines floor, gate on `process.versions.node`, or accept the noise тАФ but the comment shouldn't imply the concern is fully resolved.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** тАФ Symlink error semantics differ slightly between old code and fs.cpSync тАФ The old code dereferenced symlinks implicitly (via `fs.statSync` and `fs.copyFileSync`). `fs.cpSync` defaults to `symlinks: false` which also dereferences, so common cases match. Divergence appears only if a `public/` dir or template contains a symlink whose target is missing or is itself a directory тАФ `cpSync`'s symlink error paths differ from `copyFileSync`'s. For Vite's public-dir and create-vite templates this is almost certainly a non-issue (symlinks aren't expected), but it's worth confirming no template/public dir ships symlinks.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

Two warnings and two info-level flags. The primary concern is that removing the hand-rolled `copyDir` drops a guard that silently handled the case where `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir`; `fs.cpSync` will throw in that scenario, converting a previously-successful config into a hard error. The secondary warning is that `fs.cpSync` emits an `ExperimentalWarning` on Node < 22.3.0, which is within Vite/create-vite's declared support range, and the eslint-disable comments dismiss this rather than address it. An info-level note flags a minor symlink error-path divergence that is likely non-issue for these call sites.

**5** finding(s): ЁЯФ┤ 0  ┬╖  ЁЯЯб 3  ┬╖  ЁЯФ╡ 2

- ЁЯЯб `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ Guard removal creates behavior regression when outDir is nested inside publicDir тАФ The deleted `copyDir` in `utils.ts` contained a guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) that skipped entries inside `publicDir` whose resolved path equals `outDir` тАФ the case where `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir` (e.g. `publicDir: 'public'`, `outDir: 'public/build'`). The old behavior silently skipped the nested outDir entry. `fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true })` does not reproduce this: when the destination is inside the source, `cpSync` throws `ERR_FS_CP_DIR_TO_SUBDIRECTORY`. This converts a previously-successful config into a hard error during `closeBundle`. Either confirm an upstream guard exists in `prepareOutDirPlugin`, or replicate the check before calling `cpSync`.
- ЁЯЯб `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ fs.cpSync is experimental on supported Node versions (< 22.3.0) тАФ eslint-disable silences the real concern тАФ `fs.cpSync` carried Stability:1 (Experimental) status and emitted a runtime `ExperimentalWarning` until Node 22.3.0. Vite declares support for Node 18/20/22, and create-vite runs on whatever Node the user has. Users on Node 18.xтАУ22.2 will see `ExperimentalWarning: fs.cpSync is an experimental feature` on every build's public-dir copy and every scaffolding run. The eslint-disable comment ('it is not experimental in Node 22.3+') is true but doesn't address the supported-versions floor. Either bump the engines floor, gate on `process.versions.node`, or accept the noise тАФ but the comment shouldn't imply the concern is fully resolved.
- ЁЯЯб `create-vite/src/index.ts:1` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ fs.cpSync is experimental on supported Node versions (< 22.3.0) тАФ eslint-disable silences the real concern тАФ Same issue as in `prepareOutDir.ts`: `fs.cpSync` emits a runtime `ExperimentalWarning` on Node < 22.3.0, which is within create-vite's supported Node range. The eslint-disable comment dismisses the concern without addressing it for users on older supported Node versions.
- ЁЯФ╡ `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ Symlink error semantics differ slightly between old code and fs.cpSync тАФ The old code dereferenced symlinks implicitly (via `fs.statSync` and `fs.copyFileSync`). `fs.cpSync` defaults to `symlinks: false` which also dereferences, so common cases match. Divergence appears only if a `public/` dir or template contains a symlink whose target is missing or is itself a directory тАФ `cpSync`'s symlink error paths differ from `copyFileSync`'s. For Vite's public-dir and create-vite templates this is almost certainly a non-issue (symlinks aren't expected), but it's worth confirming no template/public dir ships symlinks.
- ЁЯФ╡ `create-vite/src/index.ts:1` _[Correctness, Security]_ тАФ Symlink error semantics differ slightly between old code and fs.cpSync тАФ Same info-level note as in `prepareOutDir.ts`: the old code's implicit symlink dereference via `fs.statSync`/`fs.copyFileSync` and `cpSync`'s default `symlinks: false` match for common cases, but diverge for edge-case symlinks (missing targets, directory symlinks). Worth confirming create-vite templates don't ship symlinks.

---
_Reviewed by: Correctness, Security ┬╖ glm-5.2 ┬╖ findings reflect the enabled lenses only_

The eslint-disable comment dismisses the concern without addressing it for users on older supported Node versions.
